homepage.heroSubline: We build and restore the brick and stone foundations that define Philly. From historic row homes to modern builds, we handle what others won't.
aboutText: Summit Masonry started in 2012 with a pickup truck and a passion for preserving Philadelphia's architectural heritage. Twelve years later, we're still family-owned, just with 20+ masons who treat every tuckpointing job and foundation repair like it's their own home.

Philadelphia masonry isn't just about mortar - it's about understanding freeze-thaw cycles, matching 19th century brick blends, and working on tight rowhouse lots. We train our team in traditional techniques and modern solutions because these walls need to last another 100 years.

When we're not rebuilding brownstone stoops in Rittenhouse or repairing chimneys in Chestnut Hill, you'll find us sponsoring neighborhood preservation societies and teaching masonry workshops at the Community College of Philadelphia.

valueProps.title: Historic Material Specialists
valueProps.description: We source period-correct brick and stone to maintain your property's original character.

valueProps.title: Freeze-Thaw Experts
valueProps.description: Our mortar mixes are engineered for Philadelphia's extreme temperature swings.

valueProps.title: Tight Space Pros
valueProps.description: Alley access? Shared walls? We've handled every challenging rowhome scenario.

valueProps.title: No Surprise Pricing
valueProps.description: Fixed-rate quotes with detailed scopes - what we promise is what you pay.

homepage.ctaHeadline: Need masonry that stands up to Philly?
homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-MAS-R91 today for a free assessment. We'll show up on time, give you straight answers, and get it done right.

services.name: Brick Repointing
services.shortDescription: Restore crumbling mortar joints with blends that match your home's original character.
services.description:
Philadelphia's freeze-thaw cycles destroy mortar joints faster than anywhere else. We don't just slap on modern mortar - our masons analyze your existing composition, then custom blend lime-based mixes that match the original color and flexibility.

For historic districts like Society Hill, we use hand tools to carefully rake out deteriorated mortar without damaging fragile bricks. Modern buildings get polymer-fortified mixes that resist salt damage from winter roads. Every job starts with a moisture assessment to address underlying issues before they become expensive repairs.
process: Step 1: Diagnostic evaluation of mortar composition and wall stability
Step 2: Custom mortar mixing (color-matched if needed)
Step 3: Precision joint raking and cleaning
Step 4: Hand-applied repointing with period-appropriate techniques
benefits: Prevents water infiltration that leads to interior damage
Maintains structural integrity of older buildings

faq.question: How often should Philly rowhomes be repointed?
faq.answer: Most need work every 25-40 years, but south-facing walls exposed to road salt may need attention every 15 years. We'll tell you what your specific building requires.

faq.question: Will repointing change my brick's appearance?
faq.answer: Not if done right. We take mortar samples to our lab for precise color matching, and use techniques that avoid smearing or staining the brick faces.

services.name: Chimney Repair
services.shortDescription: Stop leaks and restore stability to Philadelphia's vulnerable chimneys.
services.description:
Nor'easters wreck havoc on Philadelphia chimneys - we see crumbling crowns, spalling brick, and deteriorated flue liners daily. Our repairs go beyond cosmetic fixes to address the root causes.

For Victorian twins in West Philly, we rebuild corbeled brick details using salvaged materials when possible. Modern fireplaces get stainless steel liners sized precisely for your heating appliance. Every repair includes flashing inspections (the #1 leak source) and waterproofing treatments that resist wind-driven rain.

We're one of few contractors certified to work on National Register properties, with experience maintaining chimneys at landmark buildings like the Union League.
process: Step 1: Structural inspection with chimney camera
benefits: Prevents costly water damage to interiors

faq.question: How do I know if my chimney needs repair?
faq.answer: Look for mortar debris in your fireplace, white staining (efflorescence), or visible cracks. Any chimney over 20 years old should be inspected annually - salt air from the Delaware accelerates deterioration.

faq.question: Can you match my intricate brick patterns?
faq.answer: We maintain an inventory of period bricks from Philadelphia demolitions, and custom tool replacement pieces when needed. Last year we perfectly replicated 1890s dogtooth patterns on a Powelton Village home.

services.name: Foundation Repair
services.shortDescription: Stabilize settling rowhomes with minimally invasive techniques.
services.description:
Philadelphia's clay soil and century-old foundations are a bad combination. We specialize in solutions that don't require demolishing your historic basement - helical piers, carbon fiber straps, and precision grout injection.

In Fishtown and Northern Liberties, we see recurring issues with rubble stone foundations shifting under added rooftop decks. Our engineers design repairs that account for shared walls and tight lot lines. For newer construction, we address improper backfill compaction that leads to basement cracks after just 5-10 years.

Every job includes a drainage assessment - often the real culprit behind foundation movement. We partner with licensed waterproofers to create complete solutions, not temporary fixes.
process: Step 1: Laser level assessment of settlement
benefits: Addresses water issues at their source

faq.question: How much do Philly rowhome foundation repairs typically cost?
faq.answer: Straightforward pier installations run $8,000-$15,000, while major reconstructions can reach $50,000+. We always explore the most cost-effective solution first - last month we stabilized a Manayunk home with grout injection for under $6,000.

faq.question: Will repairs disturb my finished basement?
faq.answer: Rarely. Most helical pier installations require only 12" access holes, which we core drill through flooring with minimal mess. Any disturbed areas are restored to original condition.

services.name: Brownstone Restoration
services.shortDescription: Bring back the grandeur of Philadelphia's iconic brownstones.
services.description:
Original brownstone façades suffer terribly from acid rain and improper repairs. Our restoration process starts with gentle cleaning using low-pressure steam and natural poultices - no sandblasting that erodes delicate surfaces.

For Rittenhouse Square and Fitler Square homes, we replicate ornamental details like quoins and window hoods using traditional stone carving techniques. Damaged sections get repaired with salvaged brownstone (we maintain a stockpile from demolished buildings) or with modern stone epoxies when appropriate.

Every project includes a customized maintenance plan - these soft sandstone surfaces need specific sealants and washing schedules. We've restored over 120 brownstones since 2015, including six on the historic register.
process: Step 1: Material analysis and cleaning tests
Step 2: Structural assessment of stone integrity

faq.question: Why not just replace damaged brownstone with modern materials?
faq.answer: Modern substitutes lack the warmth and workability of original stone. We exhaust every repair option first - our epoxy techniques can salvage seemingly hopeless sections while maintaining historic authenticity.

faq.question: How often should brownstone be maintained?
faq.answer: Annual inspections catch small issues before they become major problems. Full cleaning and resealing is typically needed every 5-8 years, more often for south-facing walls exposed to road spray.

services.name: Paver Installation
services.shortDescription: Durable brick and stone patios built for Philadelphia weather.
services.description:
Philadelphia backyards need pavers that can handle everything from summer barbecues to winter snowplows. We install with 6" gravel bases and edge restraints that prevent the heaving and spreading you see around town.

In Northern Liberties and Queen Village, we specialize in small-space solutions - herringbone patterns that visually expand tight lots, permeable designs that reduce stormwater fees. For Chestnut Hill estates, we craft bluestone terraces with hidden drainage systems that handle heavy rain.

Every installation includes a custom winter prep guide - proper de-icer selection saves thousands in replacement costs. We source materials from local suppliers like Philadelphia Brick Works to ensure colors complement your home's existing masonry.
process: Step 1: Site evaluation and drainage planning
benefits: Increases property value more than decks

faq.question: How long do paver installations last in Philly?
faq.answer: Our installations average 25+ years with proper care. The key is the base - we compact 6" of gravel, not the skimpy 2-3" layers some contractors use that fail after a few freeze-thaw cycles.

faq.question: Can pavers handle rooftop installations?
faq.answer: Absolutely - we've installed over 40 rooftop paver systems in Center City condos. These require specialized waterproofing and weight calculations that most masons don't understand. Our structural engineer signs off on every plan.

reviews.name: Meredith K.
reviews.text: Summit saved our 1890s brownstone in Graduate Hospital after a bad contractor used Portland cement that was causing spalling. They sourced matching stone from a demolished building in Powelton Village and replicated our original carvings perfectly. Two years later, the façade still looks immaculate despite being on a busy street.

reviews.name: Carlos R.
reviews.text: Our Fishtown rowhome had a 3" settlement crack running through the basement. Summit installed helical piers in one day without even needing to move our laundry machines. They pointed out how our downspouts were dumping water right at the foundation - fixed that too. No more sticking doors and the basement stayed dry all winter.

reviews.name: Eleanor W.
reviews.text: The team rebuilt our crumbling chimney in Chestnut Hill using salvaged brick that matched perfectly. They noticed our flashing was installed wrong 20 years ago - no wonder we had leaks! Finished the job by applying a clear water repellent that doesn't change the brick's appearance. Professional from start to finish.

reviews.name: Darnell T.
reviews.text: Our tiny Northern Liberties backyard is now the envy of the block thanks to Summit's paver work. They designed a pattern that makes the space feel bigger and installed a hidden drainage channel that keeps it from flooding. Even plowed the snow last winter without shifting a single brick.

reviews.name: Gina P.
reviews.text: After getting three ridiculous quotes to repoint our Society Hill row house, Summit gave us a fair price and did impeccable work. They matched the original mortar color exactly and cleaned every brick by hand. The project manager even gave us a tutorial on proper maintenance for historic brick.

reviews.name: Marcus L.
reviews.text: Summit fixed our West Philly twin's chimney after a tree limb damaged the crown. They didn't just patch it - replaced several compromised bricks and installed a proper cricket to prevent future leaks. The crew showed up exactly when they said and left the roof cleaner than they found it.

service-areas.title: Philadelphia Masonry Service Areas
service-areas.description: Serving Philadelphia and surrounding communities with expert masonry services. Local specialists in historic brick restoration.

serviceAreas.content: Summit Masonry is proud to serve Philadelphia, the heart of Pennsylvania and a city rich with historic architecture. From brickwork repairs on classic row houses to modern masonry installations for commercial properties, our team delivers top-quality craftsmanship. Whether you're in Center City or the surrounding neighborhoods, we understand the unique masonry needs of Philly's diverse buildings. Trust us to preserve and enhance the beauty of your property with durable, weather-resistant solutions.
serviceAreas.localContext: Philadelphia is known for its historic charm, featuring iconic brick rowhouses and landmarks like Independence Hall.

serviceAreas.content: Serving Conshohocken, Summit Masonry provides expert masonry services for both residential and commercial properties. Whether you need a new stone patio, brick wall repairs, or a custom fireplace, our skilled masons deliver precision and durability. Conshohocken's mix of historic and modern architecture demands versatile masonry solutions, and we’re here to meet that need. Let us help you enhance your property’s curb appeal and structural integrity.
